title: Bridge Essay: Parent-infant synchrony and the construction of shared timing; physiological pre

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Parent-infant synchrony and the construction of shared timing; physiological precursors, developmental outcomes, and risk conditions.** — J Child Psychol Psychiatry. PMID: 17355401. DOI: 10.1111/j.1469-7610.2006.01701.x. Synchrony, aconstruct used across multiple fields to denote the temporal relationship between events, is applied to the study of parent-infant interactions and suggested as amodel for intersubjectivity. Three types of timed relationships between the parent and child'saffective behavior are assessed: concurrent, sequential, and organized in an ongoing patterned format, and the development of each is charted across the first year.
